news.title: ‘Diego, give us a hand’: Argentina v England revives historic tensions
news.lede: Decades after the Falklands war and Maradona’s ‘Hand of God’ goal, the fixture is once again being discussed as far more than a game. When Argentina’s national football team burst into the dressing room after beating Switzerland 3-1, they celebrated by singing The Fourth Star, the country’s unofficial World Cup anthem. “For Malvinas, for Diego,” Lionel Messi and his teammates chanted, invoking both the Falkland Islands – known as Islas Malvinas in Argentina – and their football legend Diego Maradona. The lyrics had already become ubiquitous in Argentina during this tournament, but they took on a new significance once it was confirmed that Wednesday’s World Cup semi-final would once again pit Argentina against England.

news.title: World Cup final 2026 date and time: Spain to face England or Argentina
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. Spain will face England or Argentina in the World Cup 2026 final. La Roja became the first team to book their place in this weekend’s tournament showpiece with a 2-0 win over France in Arlington, Texas.

news.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Spain consign France to death by disruption
news.lede: The cruellest punishment Spain imposed on France was not that they upended them and progressed to the final; or that they reduced them to futile escape artistes in an airless glass box, or that they imprisoned France’s vaunted forward-line in an iron cage, but that they pushed them to utter hopelessness. Spain sucked the fight out of France, they infused an irrevocable sense of fatalism into a group smattered with high-class football artistes. A bunch that features the most expensive footballer, the Ballon D'or and Champions League winners. The match, a 2-0 victory achieved through the goals of Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro, wouldn’t be remembered for its electrifying thrills or its riveting drama or suspense, but for the tactical coup, for the flawless technical showmanship.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time but will have to settle for either third or fourth place at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals, but were defeated by Spain in the semi-finals.

news.title: How to Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup Semifinal Live Without Cable (and Even for Free)
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup semifinals are officially underway, and we'll see two nail-biting matches to decide who will compete in the final on Sunday. What started as a massive worldwide competition between 48 countries has been narrowed down to France, Spain, England, and Argentina. Today's semifinal game is between heavyweights, France and Spain , and tomorrow we'll see England vs Argentina . Which of these teams will move forward to the World Cup final, and which will compete in Saturday's bronze final?

news.title: I still feel pain of '98 but this can be different for England - Shearer
news.lede: It is 28 years since Argentina ended my World Cup dream at France '98 - and it still hurts now. I can still picture their players dancing and celebrating next to us as both teams waited to get on their buses after our epic last-16 tie. We had come so close to beating them, but we fell on the wrong side of a penalty shootout, and we were going home. I was captain and it was tough to take, not just personally but because we had an outstanding team and I felt we had an opportunity at that tournament to make a real statement on the world stage.

news.title: Who won yesterday FIFA World Cup football match? Spain vs France - Who reached the finals after intense cl
news.lede: Spain secured a commanding 2–0 victory over France in a high-stakes semi-final clash to book their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final. In a match filled with intensity, tactical discipline, and moments of brilliance, Spain proved why they are among the tournament’s most consistent sides. Goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro guided Luis de la Fuente’s team into the final, marking only the second time they have reached the tournament’s showpiece match. Who won Yesterday FIFA World Cup football matchSpain won the match against France 2-0 to secure a spot in the final.

news.title: World Cup semifinal picks, predictions: Best bets for England-Argentina
news.lede: The World Cup semifinals featured the world's top four teams: France, Spain, England and Argentina, all of which have previously won the World Cup. Spain then proceeded to beat France 2-0 on Tuesday to become the first 2026 World Cup Finalist. This was the third time that all four semifinalists had been previous champions (1970, 1990), and it was the first time that the top four teams in FIFA's rankings had all made the World Cup semifinals (since rankings began in 1992). That achievement was admittedly aided by FIFA seeding the top four teams in separate quadrants for the first time, but all four still got through the group stage and an extra knockout round to be here.
